It’s a place where new ideas, unfamiliar approaches, and foreign concepts always lose.[Read more at location 139](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=139)Add a note
		- The real world isn’t a place, it’s an excuse. It’s a justification for not trying.[Read more at location 149](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=149)Add a note
		- Learning from mistakes is overrated[Read more at location 151](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=151)Add a note
		- What do you really learn from mistakes? You might learn what not to do again, but how valuable is that? You still don’t know what you should do[Read more at location 159](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=159)Add a note
		- Contrast that with learning from your successes. Success gives you real ammunition. When something succeeds, you know what worked—and you can do it again.[Read more at location 160](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=160)Add a note
		- People who failed before have the same amount of success as people who have never tried at all.[Read more at location 164](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=164)Add a note
		- Planning is guessing[Read more at location 169](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=169)Add a note
		- Start referring to your business plans as business guesses,[Read more at location 172](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=172)Add a note
		- Now you can stop worrying about them as much. They just aren’t worth the stress.[Read more at location 173](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=173)Add a note
		- Plans are inconsistent with improvisation.[Read more at location 175](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=175)Add a note
		- You have the most information when you’re doing something, not before you’ve done it.[Read more at location 178](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=178)Add a note
		- That’s the worst time to make a big decision.[Read more at location 179](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=179)Add a note
		- Plans more than a few pages long just wind up as fossils in your file cabinet.[Read more at location 182](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=182)Add a note
		- Give up on the guesswork. Decide what you’re going to do this week, not this year. Figure out the next most important thing and do[Read more at location 183](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=183)Add a note
		- that. Make decisions right before you do something, not far in advance.[Read more at location 184](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=184)Add a note
		- People ask, “how big is your company?” It’s small talk, but they’re not looking for a small answer.[Read more at location 188](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=188)Add a note
		- Why is expansion always the goal?[Read more at location 192](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=192)Add a note
		- Do we look at Harvard or Oxford and say, “If they’d only expand and branch out and hire thousands more professors and go global and open other campuses all over the world . . . then they’d be great schools.” Of course not. That’s not how we measure the value of these institutions. So why is it the way we measure businesses?[Read more at location 194](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=194)Add a note
		- Maybe the right size for your company is five people. Maybe it[Read more at location 197](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=197)Add a note
		- forty. Maybe it’s two hundred. Or maybe it’s just you and a laptop.[Read more at location 197](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=197)Add a note
		- Grow slow and see what feels right[Read more at location 198](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=198)Add a note
		- Have you ever noticed that while small businesses wish they were bigger, big businesses dream about being more agile and flexible?[Read more at location 200](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=200)Add a note
		- once you get big, it’s really hard to shrink without firing people, damaging morale, and changing the entire way you do business.[Read more at location 201](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=201)Add a note
		- Ramping up doesn’t have to be your goal. And we’re not talking just about the number of employees you have either. It’s also true for expenses, rent, IT infrastructure, furniture, etc.[Read more at location 202](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=202)Add a note
		- Workaholics wind up creating more problems than they solve. First off, working like that just isn’t sustainable over time.[Read more at location 212](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=212)Add a note
		- Workaholics miss the point, too. They try to fix problems by throwing sheer hours at them. They try to make up for intellectual laziness with brute force.[Read more at location 214](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=214)Add a note
		- They even create crises. They don’t look for ways to be more efficient because they actually like working overtime. They enjoy feeling like heroes.[Read more at location 216](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=216)Add a note
		- who don’t stay late feel inadequate for “merely” working reasonable hours. That leads to guilt and poor morale all around. Plus, it leads to an ass-in-seat mentality—people stay late out of obligation,[Read more at location 218](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=218)Add a note
		- If all you do is work, you’re unlikely to have sound judgments.[Read more at location 220](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=220)Add a note
		- They may claim to be perfectionists, but that just means they’re wasting time fixating on inconsequential details instead of moving on to the next task.[Read more at location 222](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=222)Add a note
		- Workaholics aren’t heroes. They don’t save the day, they just use it up. The real hero is already home because she figured out a faster way to get things done.[Read more at location 224](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=224)Add a note
		- These people scratched their own itch and exposed a huge market of people who needed exactly what they needed. That’s how you should do it too. When you build what you need, you can also assess the quality of what you make quickly and directly, instead of by proxy.[Read more at location 271](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=271)Add a note
		- Best of all, this “solve your own problem” approach lets you fall in love with what you’re making. You know the problem and the value of its solution intimately.[Read more at location 277](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=277)Add a note
		- What you do is what matters, not what you think or say or plan.[Read more at location 283](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=283)Add a note
		- Ideas are cheap and plentiful. The original pitch idea is such a small part of a business that it’s almost negligible. The real question is how well you execute.[Read more at location 289](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=289)Add a note
		- We’re not talking about all-nighters[Read more at location 296](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=296)Add a note
		- or sixteen-hour days—we’re talking about squeezing out a few extra hours a week. That’s enough time to get something going.[Read more at location 296](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=296)Add a note
		- Once you do that, you’ll learn whether your excitement and interest is real or just a passing phase. If it doesn’t pan out, you just keep going to work every day like you’ve been doing all along. You didn’t risk or lose anything, other than a bit of time,[Read more at location 297](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=297)Add a note
		- The truth is most people just don’t want it bad enough.[Read more at location 300](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=300)Add a note
		- Besides, the perfect time never arrives. You’re always too young or old or busy or broke or something else. If you constantly fret about timing things perfectly, they’ll never happen.[Read more at location 302](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=302)Add a note
		- As you get going, keep in mind why you’re doing what you’re doing.[Read more at location 305](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=305)Add a note
		- You have to believe in something. You need to have a backbone. You need to know what you’re willing to fight for. And then you need to show the world.[Read more at location 307](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=307)Add a note
		- A strong stand is how you attract superfans. They point to you and defend you. And they spread the word further, wider, and more passionately than any advertising could.[Read more at location 308](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=308)Add a note
		- If no one’s upset by what you’re saying, you’re probably not pushing hard enough. (And you’re probably boring, too.)[Read more at location 311](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=311)Add a note
		- We’re willing to lose some customers if it means that others love our products intensely. That’s our line in the sand.[Read more at location 315](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=315)Add a note
		- When you don’t know what you believe, everything becomes an argument. Everything is debatable. But when you stand for something, decisions are obvious.[Read more at location 317](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=317)Add a note
		- Standing for something isn’t just about writing it down. It’s about believing it and living it.[Read more at location 348](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=348)Add a note
		- If you’re building something like a factory or restaurant, then you may indeed need that outside cash. But a lot of companies don’t need expensive infrastructure—especially these days.[Read more at location 352](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=352)Add a note
		- You give up control. When you turn to outsiders[Read more at location 357](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=357)Add a note
		- Are you starting your own business to take orders from someone else? Raise money and that’s what you’ll wind up doing.[Read more at location 359](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=359)Add a note
		- “Cashing out” begins to trump building a quality business. Investors want their money back—and quickly (usually three to five years). Long-term sustainability goes out the window when those involved only want to cash out as soon as they can.[Read more at location 360](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=360)Add a note
		- It’s usually a bad deal. When you’re just beginning, you have no leverage. That’s a terrible time to enter into any financial transaction.[Read more at location 366](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=366)Add a note
		- Customers move down the totem pole. You wind up building what investors want instead of what customers want.[Read more at location 367](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=367)Add a note
		- You need less than you think[Read more at location 376](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=376)Add a note
		- Do you really need six months or can you make something in two?[Read more at location 379](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=379)Add a note
		- Do you really need an accountant or can you use Quicken and do it yourself?[Read more at location 384](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=384)Add a note
		- Do you really need a full-time support person or can you handle inquiries on your own?[Read more at location 385](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=385)Add a note
		- The start up is a magical place. It’s a place where expenses are someone else’s problem. It’s a place where that pesky thing called revenue is never an issue.[Read more at location 396](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=396)Add a note
		- A business without a path to profit isn’t a business, it’s a hobby.[Read more at location 404](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=404)Add a note
		- Building to flip is building to flop Another thing you hear a lot: “What’s your exit strategy?”[Read more at location 409](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=409)Add a note
		- Would you go into a relationship planning the breakup? Would you write the prenup on a first date? Would you meet with a divorce lawyer the morning of your wedding? That would be ridiculous, right?[Read more at location 412](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=412)Add a note
		- You need a commitment strategy, not an exit strategy.[Read more at location 414](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=414)Add a note
		- Instead of focusing on getting customers to love you, you worry about who’s going to buy you.[Read more at location 418](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=418)Add a note
		- Don’t let your business be the one that got away.[Read more at location 425](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=425)Add a note
		- the more massive an object, the more energy required to change its direction.[Read more at location 429](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=429)Add a note
		- Mass is increased by . . . Long-term contracts Excess staff Permanent decisions Meetings Thick process Inventory (physical or mental) Hardware, software, and technology lock-ins Long-term road maps Office politics[Read more at location 430](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=430)Add a note
		- The more expensive it is to make a change, the less likely you are to make it.[Read more at location 433](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=433)Add a note
		- if you keep your mass low, you can quickly change anything: your entire business model, product, feature set, and/or marketing message.[Read more at location 434](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=434)Add a note
		- Embrace constraints “I DON’T HAVE enough time/money/people/experience.” Stop whining. Less is a good thing.[Read more at location 454](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=454)Add a note
		- Writers use constraints to force creativity all the time.[Read more at location 459](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=459)Add a note
		- These days, we have more resources and people, but we still force constraints. We make sure to have only one or two people working on a product at a time. And we always keep features to a minimum. Boxing ourselves in this way prevents us from creating bloated products.[Read more at location 470](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=470)Add a note
		- Build half a product, not a half-assed product[Read more at location 474](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=474)Add a note
		- Getting to great starts by cutting out stuff that’s merely good.[Read more at location 482](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=482)Add a note
		- Start at the epicenter[Read more at location 484](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=484)Add a note
		- There’s the stuff you could do, the stuff you want to do, and the stuff you have to do. The stuff you have to do is where you should begin. Start at the epicenter.[Read more at location 485](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=485)Add a note
		- The way to find the epicenter is to ask yourself this question: “If I took this away, would what I’m selling still exist?”[Read more at location 489](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=489)Add a note
		- Then focus all your energy on making it the best it can be.[Read more at location 493](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=493)Add a note
		- Ignore the details early on[Read more at location 496](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=496)Add a note
		- Architects don’t worry about which tiles go in the shower or which brand of dishwasher to install in the kitchen until after the floor plan is finalized. They know it’s better to decide these details later.[Read more at location 496](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=496)Add a note
		- When we start designing something, we sketch out ideas with a big, thick Sharpie marker, instead of a ballpoint pen. Why? Pen points are too fine. They’re too high-resolution.[Read more at location 501](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=501)Add a note
		- Besides, you often can’t recognize the details that matter most until after you start building.[Read more at location 507](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=507)Add a note
		- Making the call is making progress[Read more at location 511](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=511)Add a note
		- Whenever you can, swap “Let’s think about it” for “Let’s decide on it.” Commit to making decisions. Don’t wait for the perfect solution. Decide and move forward.[Read more at location 513](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=513)Add a note
		- Decisions are progress. Each one you make is a brick in your foundation. You can’t build on top of “We’ll decide later,” but you can build on top of “Done.”[Read more at location 515](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=515)Add a note
		- Be a curator[Read more at location 528](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=528)Add a note
		- What makes a museum great is the stuff that’s not on the walls. Someone says no. A curator is involved, making conscious decisions about what should stay and what should go.[Read more at location 529](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=529)Add a note
		- Throw less at the problem[Read more at location 547](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=547)Add a note
		- Improving the current menu doesn’t come first. Trimming it down comes first. Then he polishes what’s left.[Read more at location 551](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=551)Add a note
		- When things aren’t working, the natural inclination is to throw more at the problem. More people, time, and money.[Read more at location 552](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=552)Add a note
		- Focus on what won’t change[Read more at location 557](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=557)Add a note
		- Amazon.com focuses on fast (or free) shipping, great selection, friendly return policies, and affordable prices. These things will always be in high demand.[Read more at location 562](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=562)Add a note
		- Japanese automakers also focus on core principles that don’t change: reliability, affordability, and practicality.[Read more at location 564](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=564)Add a note
		- Remember, fashion fades away. When you focus on permanent features, you’re in bed with things that never go out of style.[Read more at location 568](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=568)Add a note
		- Tone is in your fingers[Read more at location 570](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=570)Add a note
		- It’s tempting for people to obsess over tools instead of what they’re going to do with those tools.[Read more at location 574](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=574)Add a note

		- Meetings are toxic The worst interruptions of all are meetings.[Read more at location 713](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=713)Add a note
		- They’re usually about words and abstract concepts, not real things. They usually convey an abysmally small amount of information per minute. 		- They require thorough preparation that most people don’t have time for. They frequently have agendas so vague that nobody is really sure of the goal. One meeting leads to another meeting leads to another[Read more at location 716](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=716)Add a note
		- schedule a meeting that lasts one hour, and you invite ten people to attend. That’s actually a ten-hour meeting, not a one-hour meeting. You’re trading ten hours of productivity for one hour of meeting time. And it’s probably more like fifteen hours, because there are mental switching costs[Read more at location 723](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=723)Add a note
		- If you decide you absolutely must get together, try to make your meeting a productive one by sticking to these simple rules: Set a timer. When it rings, meeting’s over. Period. Invite as few people as possible. Always have a clear agenda. Begin with a specific problem. Meet at the site of the problem instead of a conference room. Point to real things and suggest real changes. End with a solution and make someone respons ible for implementing it.[Read more at location 728](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=728)Add a note

		- If you aren’t motivated by what you’re working on, it won’t be very good.[Read more at location 751](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=751)Add a note
		- If you absolutely have to work on long-term projects, try to dedicate one day a week (or every two weeks) to small victories that generate enthusiasm.[Read more at location 758](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=758)Add a note

		- Salatin. Salatin has a strong set of beliefs and runs his business accordingly. Polyface sells the idea that it does things a bigger agribusiness can’t do. Even though it’s more expensive to do so, it feeds cows grass instead of corn and never gives them antibiotics. It never ships food. Anyone is welcome to visit the farm anytime and go anywhere (try that at a typical meat-processing plant). Polyface doesn’t just sell chickens, it sells a way of thinking. And customers love Polyface for it. 		- Don’t confuse enthusiasm with priority Coming up with a great idea gives you a rush.[Read more at location 989](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=989)Add a note
		- By all means, have as many great ideas as you can. Get excited about them. Just don’t act in the heat of the moment. Write them down and park them for a few days. Then, evaluate their actual priority with a calm mind.[Read more at location 996](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=996)Add a note
		- Be at-home good[Read more at location 999](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=999)Add a note
		- When you get the product home, you’re actually more impressed with it than you were at the store. You live with it and grow to like it more and more. And you tell your friends, too.[Read more at location 1006](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1006)Add a note
		- When you create an at-home-good product, you may have to sacrifice a bit of in-store sizzle. A product that executes on the basics beautifully may not seem as sexy as competitors loaded with bells and whistles. Being great at a few things often doesn’t look all that flashy from afar. That’s OK. You’re aiming for a long-term relationship, not a one-night stand.[Read more at location 1008](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1008)Add a note
		- Don’t write it down How should you keep track of what customers want? Don’t. Listen, but then forget what people said. Seriously.[Read more at location 1015](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1015)Add a note
		- The requests that really matter are the ones you’ll hear over and over. After a while, you won’t be able to forget them. Your customers will be your memory.[Read more at location 1017](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1017)Add a note
		- If there’s a request that you keep forgetting, that’s a sign that it isn’t very important. The really important stuff doesn’t go away.[Read more at location 1019](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1019)Add a note
		- PROMOTION Welcome obscurity[Read more at location 1021](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1021)Add a note
		- Use this time to make mistakes without the whole world hearing about them. Keep tweaking. Work out the kinks. Test random ideas. Try new things. No one knows you, so it’s no big deal if you mess up. Obscurity helps protect your ego and preserve your confidence.[Read more at location 1024](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1024)Add a note
		- Would you want the whole world to watch you the first time you do anything?[Read more at location 1030](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1030)Add a note
		- once you do get bigger and more popular, you’re inevitably going to take fewer risks. When you’re a success, the pressure to maintain predictability and consistency builds. You get more conservative. It’s harder to take risks. That’s when things start to fossilize and change becomes difficult.[Read more at location 1033](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1033)Add a note
		- Build an audience All companies have customers. Lucky companies have fans. But the most fortunate companies have audiences.[Read more at location 1041](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1041)Add a note
		- A lot of businesses still spend big bucks to reach people. Every time they want to say something, they dip into their budgets, pull out a huge wad of cash, and place some ads. But this approach is both expensive and unreliable.[Read more at location 1043](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1043)Add a note
		- Instead of going out to reach people, you want people to come to you. An audience returns often—on its own—to see what you have to say.[Read more at location 1045](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1045)Add a note
		- build an audience. Speak, write, blog, tweet, make videos—whatever. Share information that’s valuable and you’ll slowly but surely build a loyal audience. Then when you need to get the word out, the right people will already be listening.[Read more at location 1053](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1053)Add a note
		- Out-teach your competition[Read more at location 1056](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1056)Add a note
		- Instead of trying to outspend, outsell, or outsponsor competitors, try to out-teach them. Teaching probably isn’t something your competitors are even thinking about.[Read more at location 1058](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1058)Add a note
		- Teach and you’ll form a bond you just don’t get from traditional marketing tactics. Buying people’s attention with a magazine or online banner ad is one thing. Earning their loyalty by teaching them forms a whole different connection.[Read more at location 1063](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1063)Add a note
		- Big companies can afford a Super Bowl ad; you can’t. But you can afford to teach, and that’s something they’ll never do, because big companies are obsessed with secrecy. Everything at those places has to get filtered through a lawyer and go through layers of red tape. Teaching is your chance to outmaneuver them.[Read more at location 1067](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1067)Add a note
		- Emulate chefs[Read more at location 1071](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1071)Add a note
		- why do you know these few better than others? Because they share everything they know. They put their recipes in cookbooks and show their techniques on cooking shows.[Read more at location 1073](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1073)Add a note
		- Businesses are usually paranoid and secretive. They think they have proprietary this and competitive advantage that. Maybe a rare few do, but most don’t. And those that don’t should stop acting like those that do. Don’t be afraid of sharing.[Read more at location 1075](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1075)Add a note
		- he knows those recipes and techniques aren’t enough to beat him at his own game. No one’s going to buy his cookbook, open a restaurant next door, and put him out of business.[Read more at location 1078](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1078)Note: I can giv people to fools and even habifs i have but only I have the willpowe to do all of these Edit
		- What are your “recipes”? What’s your “cookbook”? What can you tell the world about how you operate that’s informative, educational, and promotional?[Read more at location 1081](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1081)Add a note
		- Go behind the scenes[Read more at location 1084](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1084)Add a note
		- Give people a backstage pass and show them how your business works. Imagine that someone wanted to make a reality show about your business. What would they share?[Read more at location 1085](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1085)Add a note
		- Letting people behind the curtain changes your relationship with them. They’ll feel a bond with you and see you as human beings instead of a faceless company. They’ll see the sweat and effort that goes into what you sell. They’ll develop a deeper level of understanding and appreciation for what you do.[Read more at location 1094](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1094)Note: same thing as seeing the routine of tim ferriss, Victor pride and mike cernovich Edit
		- Nobody likes plastic flowers[Read more at location 1097](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1097)Add a note
		- The business world is full of “professionals” who wear the uniform and try to seem perfect. In truth, they just come off as stiff and boring. No one can relate to people like that. Don’t be afraid to show your flaws.[Read more at location 1098](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1098)Add a note
		- So talk like you really talk. Reveal things that others are unwilling to discuss. Be upfront about your shortcomings. Show the latest version of what you’re working on, even if you’re not done yet.[Read more at location 1108](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1108)Add a note
		- Spam. That’s what press releases are too: generic pitches for coverage sent out to hundreds of journalists you don’t know, hoping that one will write about you.[Read more at location 1112](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1112)Add a note
		- Instead, call someone. Write a personal note. If you read a story about a similar company or product, contact the journalist who wrote it. Pitch her with some passion, some interest, some life. Do something meaningful. Be remarkable. Stand out. Be unforgettable. That’s how you’ll get the best coverage.[Read more at location 1123](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1123)Add a note
		- Forget about the Wall Street Journal[Read more at location 1127](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1127)Add a note
		- You’re better off focusing on getting your story into a trade publication or picked up by a niche blogger.[Read more at location 1130](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1130)Add a note
		- Emulate drug dealers. Make your product so good, so addictive, so “can’t miss” that giving customers a small, free taste makes them come back with cash in hand.[Read more at location 1142](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1142)Add a note
		- This will force you to make something about your product bite-size. You want an easily digestible introduction to what you sell. This gives people a way to try it without investing any money or a lot of time.[Read more at location 1143](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1143)Add a note
		- Bakeries, restaurants, and ice cream shops have done this successfully for years. Car dealers let you test-drive cars before buying them. Software firms are also getting on board, with free trials or limited-use versions. How many other industries could benefit from the drug-dealer model?[Read more at location 1145](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1145)Add a note
		- Every word you write on your Web site is marketing.[Read more at location 1155](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1155)Add a note
		- Marketing isn’t just a few individual events. It’s the sum total of everything you do.[Read more at location 1158](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1158)Add a note
		- HIRING Do it yourself first[Read more at location 1177](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1177)Add a note
		- You’ll know what a job well done looks like. You’ll know how to write a realistic job description and which questions to ask in an interview. You’ll know whether to hire someone full-time or part-time, outsource it, or keep doing it yourself (the last is preferable, if possible).[Read more at location 1181](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1181)Add a note
		- Don’t hire for pleasure; hire to kill pain. Always ask yourself: What if we don’t hire anyone? Is that extra work that’s burdening us really necessary? Can we solve the problem with a slice of software or a change of practice instead? What if we just don’t do it?[Read more at location 1193](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1193)Add a note
		- Similarly, if you lose someone, don’t replace him immediately. See how long you can get by without that person and that position. You’ll often discover you don’t need as many people as you think.[Read more at location 1195](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1195)Add a note
		- The right time to hire is when there’s more work than you can handle for a sustained period of time. There should be things you can’t do anymore. You should notice the quality level slipping. That’s when you’re hurting.[Read more at location 1197](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1197)Add a note
		- Pass on hiring people you don’t need, even if you think that person’s a great catch. You’ll be doing your company more harm than good if you bring in talented people who have nothing important to do.[Read more at location 1203](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1203)Add a note
		- You start inventing work to keep everyone busy. Artificial work leads to artificial projects. And those artificial projects lead to real costs and complexity.[Read more at location 1205](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1205)Add a note
		- Don’t worry about “the one that got away.” It’s much worse to have people on staff who aren’t doing anything meaningful. There’s plenty of talent out there. When you do have a real need, you’ll find someone who fits well.[Read more at location 1206](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1206)Add a note
		- Hire a ton of people rapidly and a “strangers at a cocktail party” problem is exactly what you end up with. There are always new faces around, so everyone is unfailingly polite. Everyone tries to avoid any conflict or drama. No one says, “This idea sucks.” People appease instead of challenge.[Read more at location 1214](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1214)Add a note
		- you start churning out something that doesn’t offend anyone but also doesn’t make anyone fall in love.[Read more at location 1218](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1218)Add a note
		- You need to know how far you can push someone. You need to know what people really mean when they say something. So hire slowly.[Read more at location 1219](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1219)Add a note
		- That’s why the cover letter is a much better test than a resumé. You hear someone’s actual voice and are able recognize if it’s in tune with you and your company.[Read more at location 1233](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1233)Add a note
		- But after that, the curve flattens out. There’s surprisingly little difference between a candidate with six months of experience and one with six years. The real difference comes from the individual’s dedication, personality, and intelligence.[Read more at location 1242](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1242)Add a note
		- Forget about formal education[Read more at location 1248](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1248)Add a note
		- Too much time in academia can actually do you harm. Take writing, for example. When you get out of school, you have to unlearn so much[Read more at location 1257](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1257)Add a note
		- Everybody works With a small team, you need people who are going to do work, not delegate work. Everyone’s got to be producing. No one can be above the work. That means you need to avoid hiring delegators, those people who love telling others what to do. Delegators are dead weight for a small team.[Read more at location 1267](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1267)Add a note
		- Hire managers of one Managers of one are people who come up with their own goals and execute them. They don’t need heavy direction. They don’t need daily check-ins. They do what a manager would do—set the tone, assign items, determine what needs to get done, etc.—but they do it by themselves and for themselves. These people free you from oversight.[Read more at location 1274](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1274)Add a note
		- How can you spot these people? Look at their backgrounds. They have set the tone for how they’ve worked at other jobs. 		- Hire great writers[Read more at location 1282](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1282)Add a note
		- Clear writing is a sign of clear thinking. Great writers know how to communicate. They make things easy to understand. They can put themselves in someone else’s shoes. They know what to omit. 		- It’s crazy not to hire the best people just because they live far away. 		- When something bad happens, tell your customers (even if they never noticed in the first place). Don’t think you can just sweep it under the rug. 		- The message should come from the top. The highest-ranking person available should take control in a forceful way. Spread the message far and wide. Use whatever megaphone you have. 		- Speed changes everything[Read more at location 1343](https://kindle.amazon.com/your_highlights_and_notes/B003ELY7PGkindle://book?action=open&asin=B003ELY7PG&location=1343)Add a note

		- Need. Very few things actually need to get done. Instead of saying “need,” you’re better off saying “maybe” or “What do you think about this?” or “How does this sound?” or “Do you think we could get away with that?” Can’t. When you say “can’t,” you probably can. Sometimes there are even opposing can’ts: “We can’t launch it like that, because it’s not quite right” versus “We can’t spend any more time on this because we have to launch.” Both of those statements can’t be true. Or wait a minute, can they? Easy. Easy is a word that’s used to describe other people’s jobs. “That should be easy for you to do, right?” But notice how rarely people describe their own tasks as easy.
